query: Hiring resumes after a large workforce reduction A platform company reduced total headcount substantially and lowered overhead. Revenue and operating margin have recovered, while AI infrastructure and a small number of product teams now show capacity bottlenecks. Broad hiring would reverse the cost reset, but refusing all hiring could impair the highest-priority work. What resource action should the CFO recommend now, and which productivity and priority signals distinguish focused reinvestment from cost-discipline reversal?
